text: When you write code, finding mistakes is a big part of the process. Identifying bugs can be tricky, but using debugging tools like breakpoints or step-through features helps you pause the program and check what's happening. A systematic approach is key: start by reproducing the error, then isolate the problematic section. Adding logging statements is another useful strategy. By printing variable values or status messages at different points, you can see the flow of your code and spot where things go wrong. Instead of guessing, follow clear steps and use these tools to solve problems efficiently.
